title: malloc and free - memory you ask for and give back
author: Prodipta Acharjee
concept: malloc asks the system for a block of memory at run time and returns a pointer to it. free gives it back. What you malloc, you must free.
explanation: Local variables vanish when a function returns. When you need memory that outlives that, or whose size you only learn while running, you ask for it with malloc - it hands back a pointer to a fresh block. The deal is strict: every malloc needs a matching free, or the memory leaks; and using a block after you free it is a serious bug. C gives you the keys to the memory and trusts you completely - which is its whole character.

exercises.question: What does this print?
hints: a points at a block big enough for 3 ints.

exercises.question: Every malloc should be matched by a...
Using a block of memory after you've freed it is...
hints: Once freed, it isn't yours anymore.
The system may hand it to someone else.
